proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (positive main effect of pembrolizumab on PFS).
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 3.414 (on pembro) vs 3.449 (off); diff = -0.035 months, t-test p = 0.081. No significant main effect of pembrolizumab on PFS in the overall cohort.
Mean pfs_months 3.641 (on sotorasib) vs 3.319 (off); diff = +0.322 months, p = 1.6e-53. Significant positive main effect.
Mean pfs_months 3.414 (on olaparib) vs 3.439 (off); diff = -0.025, p = 0.255. No significant main effect.
Mean pfs_months 3.434 (on osimertinib) vs 3.431 (off); diff = +0.003, p = 0.87. No significant main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative association).
Patients with stage_iv == 1 have shorter pfs_months than those with stage_iv == 0.
Patients with has_brain_mets == 1 have shorter pfs_months than those with has_brain_mets == 0.
Female patients (sex_female == 1) have different mean pfs_months than males.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG 0 mean PFS 4.33; ECOG 1 = 3.19 (coef -1.14, p<1e-300); ECOG 2 = 2.15 (coef -2.17, p<1e-300). Strongly negative monotonic association.
Stage IV mean PFS 2.93 vs 4.36; diff = -1.44 months, p ~ 0. Strongly negative.
Brain mets mean PFS 2.74 vs 3.66; diff = -0.92, p ~ 0. Strongly negative.
Female mean PFS 3.32 vs male 3.52; diff = -0.204, p = 2.4e-24. Females have modestly shorter PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive correlation in this cohort).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months in the overall cohort.
Squamous histology is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.757 (p ~ 0); age decile means rise monotonically from 0.74 (<52y) to 6.55 (>77y). Very strong positive association — older patients have markedly longer PFS in this cohort, opposite to typical clinical expectation.
r = 0.099, p = 2.8e-109. Modest positive association.
r = -0.127, p = 7.5e-179. Modest negative association.
r = 0.006, p = 0.16. No meaningful association of PD-L1 TPS with PFS overall.
Squamous mean PFS 2.81 vs adeno 3.67; diff = -0.86, p ~ 0. Strongly negative.
Means: current 2.99, former 3.61, never 3.67. Current smokers have ~0.6-month shorter PFS than ever-others.

proposed_hypotheses.text: egfr_mutation == 1 is associated with different (longer) pfs_months than egfr_mutation == 0.
kras_g12c == 1 is associated with longer pfs_months than kras_g12c == 0.
alk_fusion == 1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than alk_fusion == 0.
stk11_mutation is associated with shorter pfs_months.
brca2_mutation is associated with shorter pfs_months.
tmb_high is associated with longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: EGFR mut PFS 3.60 vs WT 3.41; diff = +0.19, p = 1.9e-10. Modest positive.
KRAS G12C+ PFS 4.11 vs WT 3.33; diff = +0.78, p = 4.8e-151. Strong positive (likely partly mediated by sotorasib in carriers).
ALK+ PFS 2.66 vs ALK- 3.47; diff = -0.81, p = 2.9e-69. Strongly negative.
STK11 mut 3.44 vs WT 3.43; diff = +0.009, p = 0.75. Null.
BRCA2 mut 3.32 vs WT 3.44; diff = -0.119, p = 0.041. Marginal negative.
TMB-high 3.30 vs TMB-low 3.49; diff = -0.19, p = 9.1e-18. Significant but in the OPPOSITE direction from typical IO-treatment expectation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with kras_g12c == 1, with little or no effect in kras_g12c == 0 (positive sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction).
treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with brca2_mutation == 1 (positive olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction).
treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months specifically in patients with egfr_mutation == 1 (positive osimertinib × egfr_mutation interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit increases with pdl1_tps (positive pembrolizumab × pdl1_tps interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is greater in tmb_high == 1 patients (positive pembrolizumab × tmb_high interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Cell means: WT/off 3.33, WT/on 3.33 (no effect); KRAS+/off 3.21, KRAS+/on 5.77. Interaction coef = +2.558, p ~ 0. Sotorasib effect is essentially confined to KRAS G12C+ patients.
Interaction coef = +0.037, p = 0.78. Olaparib does NOT benefit BRCA2-mutant patients. In BRCA2+: on 3.32, off 3.31 (essentially identical).
Interaction coef = -0.020, p = 0.76. No osimertinib benefit in EGFR+ (on 3.59 vs off 3.60). Surprising given clinical expectation.
Interaction coef = -0.116, p = 0.22. No positive synergy of pembrolizumab with PD-L1 expression; if anything trend is negative.
Interaction coef = -0.018, p = 0.69. No interaction; TMB-high patients have lower PFS regardless of pembrolizumab.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_osimertinib has a positive interaction with alk_fusion: patients with alk_fusion == 1 get more PFS benefit from osimertinib than alk_fusion == 0 patients.
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit is reduced in stk11_mutation == 1 patients (negative pembrolizumab × stk11_mutation interaction), reflecting reported IO resistance.
treatment_pembrolizumab benefit varies by smoking_status (e.g., greater benefit in current/former smokers than never).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.238, p = 0.017. Crude diff in ALK+ subgroup: on 2.82 vs off 2.59 = +0.23 months. Adjusted within ALK+ (controls for age/ECOG/stage/etc.): coef = +0.039, p = 0.049. Small, possibly chance, signal in ALK fusion carriers.
Interaction coef = -0.016, p = 0.77. No STK11-modulated pembrolizumab effect.
Pembro × former coef = -0.019 (p=0.68); pembro × never coef = +0.021 (p=0.74). No meaningful interaction with smoking status.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.5, treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab.
Among patients with pdl1_tps >= 0.7, treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months relative to no pembrolizumab.
Among adenocarcinoma never-smokers with pdl1_tps >= 0.5 (a canonical IO-favorable subgroup), pembrolizumab improves pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: n=10749. On pembro 3.41 vs off 3.48; diff = -0.07, p = 0.11. No benefit of pembrolizumab in PD-L1 >=50% subgroup.
n=1380. On pembro 3.36 vs off 3.65; diff = -0.28, p = 0.018. Pembrolizumab is associated with WORSE PFS at very high PD-L1 (opposite of clinical expectation).

analyses.code: ttest_ind on adeno never-smokers with PD-L1 >= 0.5
analyses.result_summary: n=1505. On 3.665 vs off 3.684; diff = -0.019, p = 0.86. No benefit even in the most IO-favorable canonical subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with brca2_mutation == 1 and not receiving any other targeted therapy, olaparib improves pfs_months.
The olaparib × brca2_mutation interaction differs by sex_female (e.g., olaparib helps BRCA2+ females specifically).
Among EGFR+ patients with adenocarcinoma histology (or never smokers), osimertinib improves pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on brca2+ no other treatments
analyses.result_summary: n=350. On 3.15 (n=99) vs off 3.49 (n=251); diff = -0.35, p = 0.16. No benefit; if anything olaparib trends worse in BRCA2+ monotherapy slice.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_olaparib * brca2_mutation', data=df[df.sex_female==s]).fit() for s in 0,1
analyses.result_summary: Females: olap × brca2 coef = +0.086, p = 0.63. Males: coef = -0.010, p = 0.96. No sex-specific olaparib benefit.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on egfr+ adeno; egfr+ never-smoker; egfr+ no brain mets
analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ adeno: on 3.68 vs off 3.72, p=0.47; EGFR+ never-smoker: 3.66 vs 3.66, p=0.90; EGFR+ no brain mets: 3.80 vs 3.83, p=0.66. Osimertinib does not benefit EGFR-mutant subsets.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction is itself modified by sex_female: among kras_g12c+ patients, sotorasib improves pfs_months in males (sex_female == 0) but NOT in females (sex_female == 1).
The sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction is reduced in patients with has_brain_mets == 1 (i.e., brain metastases attenuate sotorasib's KRAS-specific benefit).

analyses.result_summary: Triple-interaction coef = -4.688, p ~ 0. In males (sex=0): KRAS+/sotorasib mean 7.88 vs KRAS+/no-sot 3.23 (within-sex interaction = +4.66, p ~ 0). In females (sex=1): KRAS+/sotorasib 3.18 vs KRAS+/no-sot 3.19 (within-sex interaction = -0.024, p = 0.78). Sotorasib's KRAS-specific benefit is ENTIRELY confined to males.

Without brain mets: sot × kras = +2.65, p ~ 0; KRAS+/sot mean 6.04 vs KRAS+/off 3.41 (delta +2.63). With brain mets: sot × kras = +2.30, p = 9e-86; KRAS+/sot 4.93 vs KRAS+/off 2.61 (delta +2.32). Brain mets reduce absolute PFS in all cells but the interaction remains very strong.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among males with kras_g12c == 1 (sex_female == 0 & kras_g12c == 1), treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by approximately 4-5 months relative to no sotorasib.
Among females with kras_g12c == 1 (sex_female == 1 & kras_g12c == 1), treatment_sotorasib has NO effect on pfs_months.
The male-KRAS-G12C-specific sotorasib effect persists after adjustment for age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, histology, smoking status, and other treatments.

analyses.result_summary: n=3508 (1237 sotorasib, 2271 no sotorasib). Sotorasib mean PFS 7.88 vs no-sotorasib 3.23, diff = +4.644 months, p ~ 0.
n=2860 (1009 sotorasib, 1851 no sotorasib). Sotorasib 3.18 vs no-sotorasib 3.19, diff = -0.012 months, p = 0.88. Truly null.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_sotorasib + age + ecog_ps + stage_iv + has_brain_mets + albumin_g_dl + weight_loss_pct_6mo + C(histology) + C(smoking_status) + tmb_high + pdl1_tps + stk11_mutation + brca2_mutation + alk_fusion + egfr_mutation + treatment_pembrolizumab + treatment_olaparib + treatment_osimertinib', data=male_kras+).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted sotorasib coefficient = +4.584 months (p ~ 0). All baseline covariates retain expected signs. Effect is robust to adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within male KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib PFS benefit is similar across all clinical strata (ECOG 0/1/2, stage IV vs not, brain mets vs not, adeno vs squamous, STK11 mut vs WT).
Within female KRAS G12C+ patients, no clinical or genomic subgroup recovers a meaningful sotorasib effect.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest_ind in male KRAS+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib effect (mean PFS difference): ECOG 0 +4.57, ECOG 1 +4.77, ECOG 2 +4.53, stage_iv=0 +4.71, stage_iv=1 +4.62, brain_mets=0 +4.70, brain_mets=1 +4.47, adeno +4.67, squamous +4.64, STK11 WT +4.67, STK11 mut +4.49. All p < 1e-60. Effect size is highly consistent (~+4.5 to +4.8) across strata.

analyses.code: Loop over modifiers in female KRAS+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: No interaction tested in female KRAS+ subgroup reaches p<0.05; sotorasib effect remains essentially zero across all explored modifiers (ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, tmb_high, pdl1_tps, histology, smoking_status).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab × kras_g12c interaction is negative (pembro is less helpful in KRAS+ patients).
Pembrolizumab is associated with worse PFS specifically in patients with stage_iv == 0 (non-stage-IV KRAS+ subgroup).

analyses.result_summary: Pembro × kras_g12c coef = -0.113, p = 0.057. Borderline negative interaction.

analyses.code: ols('pfs_months ~ treatment_pembrolizumab * kras_g12c * stage_iv', data=df).fit() and stratified ttest
analyses.result_summary: Triple-interaction coef +0.452, p = 0.00013. Crude pembro effect in KRAS+ stage_iv=0: -0.458, p = 0.00015 (n=2265). After adjustment for age, ECOG, brain mets, sex, albumin, weight loss, histology, smoking, and other treatments: pembro coef = -0.101, p = 0.058 (much attenuated). Likely a chance/confounding finding rather than a real harm.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists at least one clinically interpretable subgroup defined by 1-2 features in which pembrolizumab significantly improves pfs_months.
There exists at least one clinically interpretable subgroup defined by 1-2 features in which olaparib significantly improves pfs_months.
There exists at least one clinically interpretable subgroup defined by 1-2 features in which osimertinib significantly improves pfs_months.

analyses.code: Exhaustive 2-binary-feature subgroup search; report top positive subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Top positive 2-feature subgroups for pembro: stk11+/brca2+ diff +0.32 (p=0.24), brca2+/pdl1>=0.5 +0.31 (p=0.21), egfr+/current_smoker +0.24 (p=0.26). None reach significance after appropriate caution. Top negative subgroups: stage_iv==0 & kras+ (-0.46, p=2e-4), alk+/squamous (-0.37, p=0.03). No reliable positive subgroup for pembrolizumab.

Top positive: stk11+/brca2+ diff +0.36 (p=0.25), egfr+/stk11+ +0.33 (p=0.026, n=980), alk+/pdl1>=0.5 +0.28 (p=0.17). The egfr+/stk11+ signal is small and not biologically expected; likely chance. No reliable subgroup.

Top positive: kras+/alk+ +0.665 (p=0.03, n=259, very small), kras+/stk11+ +0.42 (p=0.04, n=896), no_brain_mets/alk+ +0.29 (p=0.012, n=1843), stage_iv=1/alk+ +0.28 (p=0.012). Only ALK-fusion-positive strata show consistent (small) positive osimertinib signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with alk_fusion == 1, treatment_osimertinib is associated with a small but real positive effect on pfs_months (~+0.2 to +0.25 months crude).
The osimertinib × alk_fusion effect does not differ by sex_female (i.e., it is present in both sexes).

analyses.code: ols within ALK+ subgroup with full adjustment
analyses.result_summary: ALK+ crude osimertinib mean PFS 2.82 vs 2.59, diff +0.231, p = 0.018. Adjusted (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, albumin, weight loss, sex, histology, smoking): coef = +0.039, p = 0.049. Effect shrinks substantially with adjustment; likely small or chance.

analyses.code: Stratified ttest by sex within ALK+
analyses.result_summary: Males ALK+: osimertinib diff +0.243, p = 0.075 (n=1343). Females ALK+: +0.243, p = 0.079 (n=1121). Effect is symmetric in both sexes.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among male KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib effect is larger in older patients (positive sotorasib × age_years interaction).
Among male KRAS G12C+ patients, concomitant pembrolizumab attenuates the sotorasib benefit (negative sotorasib × pembrolizumab interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = +0.030 per year, p = 2e-9. Sotorasib benefit is amplified by age: at mean age the effect is ~+4.6 months, increasing by ~0.03 month per additional year. Could partly reflect age main-effect on PFS.
pembro × sotorasib coef = -0.370, p = 0.023. Within male KRAS G12C+, adding pembrolizumab to sotorasib slightly reduces the benefit. Marginal — possible chance or interference.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab has no statistically supported interaction with any single feature on the natural scale (no subgroup-defining modifier survives a strict screen).
Olaparib has no statistically supported interaction with any single feature.
Osimertinib has no statistically supported interaction with any single feature except a small positive interaction with alk_fusion.
Sotorasib's strongest single-feature interaction is with kras_g12c (huge positive); the next strongest is with sex_female (negative), driven entirely through the kras_g12c subgroup.

analyses.code: ols treatment*feature for each numeric/cat feature, screen p<0.01
analyses.result_summary: Only weakly significant pembro interactions are with stage_iv (+0.108, p=0.007) and weight_loss_pct_6mo (+0.018, p=3e-4). Both are tiny and do not produce a positive subgroup. Confirms no real pembrolizumab heterogeneity.
No olaparib × feature interaction reaches p<0.01. Confirms olaparib has no detectable heterogeneity in this cohort.
Only osimertinib × alk_fusion shows a borderline interaction (+0.238, p=0.017). No other feature × osimertinib interaction is significant at p<0.01.

Sotorasib × kras_g12c +2.558 (p ~ 0); sotorasib × sex_female -0.562 (p=5e-41); sotorasib × alk_fusion -0.488 (p=4e-7); sotorasib × egfr_mutation -0.241 (p=1e-4); sotorasib × histology[squamous] +0.171 (p=2e-4); sotorasib × smoking[never] -0.50 (p=3e-14). All non-KRAS terms are mediated by the KRAS subgroup composition.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup definition for sotorasib's PFS benefit is `kras_g12c == 1 AND sex_female == 0` (male KRAS G12C+); no additional predicate (ECOG, stage, brain mets, histology, smoking, STK11, age) is required to capture the heterogeneity.

analyses.code: Compare sotorasib effect across substrata of male KRAS+ vs adding more predicates
analyses.result_summary: Adding additional predicates does not materially shrink residual effect: male KRAS+ overall +4.64; male KRAS+ adeno +4.67; male KRAS+ squamous +4.64; male KRAS+ ECOG 0 +4.57; male KRAS+ ECOG 2 +4.53; male KRAS+ stage_iv=0 +4.71; male KRAS+ stage_iv=1 +4.62; male KRAS+ no brain mets +4.70; male KRAS+ brain mets +4.47. The two-predicate definition (kras_g12c==1 AND sex_female==0) is sufficient and parsimonious.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatments do not interact strongly with each other on PFS in the overall cohort (no large joint-treatment synergy or antagonism).

analyses.code: Pairwise treatment*treatment interaction tests in full cohort
analyses.result_summary: Pairwise treatment-by-treatment interaction terms in the overall cohort all have small coefficients (<0.05 magnitude) and high p-values (>0.1) except the male KRAS+ specific pembro × sotorasib effect (-0.37, p=0.023) within that subgroup. No clinically meaningful drug-drug interaction in the overall cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong positive correlation between age_years and pfs_months (r ~ 0.76) is not explained by treatment differences across age — it reflects baseline cohort structure.

analyses.code: Adjusted multivariable model: pfs ~ age + ecog + stage + brain mets + albumin + ... + treatment terms
analyses.result_summary: In a fully adjusted multivariable model, age_years coefficient = +0.168 months/year (p ~ 0). Each year of age associates with ~5 days of additional PFS controlling for ECOG, stage, brain mets, labs, treatments. This is opposite to normal clinical intuition and reflects how the dataset was generated.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Independent baseline predictors of shorter pfs_months in this cohort include: higher ecog_ps, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, squamous histology, current smoking, lower albumin, greater weight loss, sex_female, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation. Independent positive predictors include: higher age_years, kras_g12c (largely via sotorasib effect), egfr_mutation.

analyses.code: Full multivariable OLS on whole cohort with all features and treatments
analyses.result_summary: Significant adjusted coefficients: ECOG -1.10/level, stage_iv -1.42, brain_mets -0.91, squamous -0.72, current_smoker is reference, former_smoker +0.55, never_smoker +0.55 (vs current), albumin +0.46/(g/dL), weight_loss_pct_6mo -0.073/%, sex_female -0.21, alk_fusion -0.029 (NS), brca2_mutation -0.111, kras_g12c +0.89 (largely treatment-mediated), egfr_mutation +0.001 (NS), age +0.168/year. Treatments (main effects) all near zero in adjusted model. STK11_mutation -0.019 (p=0.038, marginal).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic treatment-effect heterogeneity search (treatment × feature interaction screen plus 2-variable subgroup search) for treatment_pembrolizumab finds NO subgroup with a meaningful, robust positive effect on pfs_months.
A systematic treatment-effect heterogeneity search for treatment_olaparib finds NO subgroup with a meaningful, robust positive effect on pfs_months.

A systematic treatment-effect heterogeneity search for treatment_osimertinib finds at most a small (~+0.03 to +0.25 months) positive effect confined to alk_fusion == 1 patients; no other subgroup shows benefit.

A systematic treatment-effect heterogeneity search for treatment_sotorasib confirms one large, robust subgroup: kras_g12c == 1 AND sex_female == 0 (male KRAS G12C+), with ~+4.5 to +4.7 months PFS benefit. No additional subgroup-defining features further refine this.

analyses.code: Combined screen: treatment×feature interactions + exhaustive 2-binary subgroup grid + canonical subgroups (PD-L1, TMB, ECOG, histology, smoking)
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab's largest crude positive subgroup (stk11+/brca2+, +0.32) is not statistically significant (p=0.24) and is in a tiny stratum (n=231). All canonical IO subgroups (PD-L1>=50%, PD-L1>=70%, adeno never-smoker high PD-L1) show NO benefit; the very-high PD-L1 subgroup actually shows a small negative effect (-0.28, p=0.018). Conclusion: no subgroup with a real positive pembrolizumab effect.

Olaparib's largest crude positive subgroup is egfr+/stk11+ (+0.33, p=0.026, n=980); biologically implausible and unlikely to reflect a true effect. All BRCA2+ subgroups (overall, by sex, by no-other-treatment) show no benefit. No reliable positive olaparib subgroup.

Only osimertinib × alk_fusion produces consistent (small) positive signals across multiple substrata: ALK+ overall +0.231 crude (p=0.018), no_brain_mets/ALK+ +0.287, stage_iv=1/ALK+ +0.285. Adjusted effect within ALK+ only +0.039 (p=0.049). EGFR+ subgroups show no benefit. Effect is biologically unexpected (osimertinib targets EGFR, not ALK) and is small.

Top 2-feature subgroup: sex_female==0 & kras_g12c+ with diff +4.644 months (p ~ 0). Adding 3rd-level conditioning (ECOG, stage, brain mets, histology, smoking, STK11) does not further shrink or improve the subgroup. Female KRAS G12C+ patients do NOT benefit (-0.012, p=0.88). Triple interaction sot×kras×sex coef = -4.69 (p ~ 0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab has no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup of this NSCLC cohort (no defining predicate yields a positive treatment effect).
FINAL: treatment_olaparib has no PFS benefit in any identifiable subgroup, including BRCA2-mutant patients.
FINAL: treatment_osimertinib has at most a small positive PFS effect (~+0.04 to +0.25 months) confined to alk_fusion == 1 patients; the canonical egfr_mutation subgroup shows no benefit.

FINAL: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by approximately +4.6 months ONLY in patients with kras_g12c == 1 AND sex_female == 0 (male KRAS G12C+); the female-half of the KRAS G12C+ population (sex_female == 1) shows zero benefit. The complete subgroup definition is the conjunction of these two predicates.

analyses.result_summary: Across exhaustive screens, no subgroup produces a positive, multiple-testing-robust pembrolizumab effect. Best candidates produce diff < +0.32 with p > 0.20.
No subgroup shows a robust positive olaparib effect; BRCA2+ patients on olaparib have nearly identical PFS to BRCA2+ off olaparib (+0.04, p=0.78).
Only ALK fusion subgroup produces a small positive signal (+0.23 crude, +0.04 adjusted, p ~0.018-0.049); no benefit in EGFR+ canonical subgroup.

analyses.code: Aggregation of iterations 1, 5, 9, 10, 11, 17, 21 culminating in subgroup definition
analyses.result_summary: Within male KRAS G12C+ (n=3508): sotorasib +4.644 months crude / +4.584 adjusted (p ~ 0). Within female KRAS G12C+ (n=2860): -0.012 (p=0.88). The interaction triple sot × kras × sex_female has coef = -4.69, p ~ 0. Subgroup definition is complete with two predicates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The sotorasib/male-KRAS finding is not driven by lab-value imbalance: it persists when adjusting for hemoglobin, alkaline_phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium, ldh, crp, nlr.

analyses.code: ols full lab adjustment in male KRAS+ subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Adjusting for the full lab panel in addition to clinical features within male KRAS G12C+ leaves the sotorasib coefficient near +4.58 (p ~ 0). Lab values explain effectively none of the heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After exhaustive screening, no other treatment shows clinically meaningful heterogeneity. The dataset contains exactly one major treatment-effect heterogeneity signal: sotorasib in male KRAS G12C+.

analyses.result_summary: Pembro/olap/osi do not produce a >0.5-month gain in any single- or two-feature subgroup with p<0.01 robustness. The sotorasib male-KRAS effect is roughly 20× larger than any other treatment-subgroup effect in the cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final summary: (a) treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by ~+4.6 months in patients with kras_g12c == 1 AND sex_female == 0; no effect elsewhere. (b) treatment_osimertinib has a small positive effect (~+0.04 to +0.23 months) confined to alk_fusion == 1; no effect in egfr_mutation+. (c) treatment_pembrolizumab has no positive effect in any subgroup. (d) treatment_olaparib has no positive effect in any subgroup, including brca2_mutation+. Baseline negative prognostic features include higher ecog_ps, stage_iv==1, has_brain_mets==1, squamous histology, current smoking, lower albumin, greater weight_loss_pct_6mo, sex_female==1, alk_fusion==1, brca2_mutation==1. Higher age_years is positively correlated with pfs_months in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Confirmed across systematic screening. Best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis for the cohort is sotorasib + male KRAS G12C+ (signed effect = +4.64 months PFS, p ~ 0); secondary smaller signal is osimertinib + ALK fusion (+0.23 months, p=0.018). Pembrolizumab and olaparib have no supported beneficial subgroup.
